key_insight: A time-aware tabular deep learning model can accurately predict inter-stop trolleybus energy use while also quantifying which operational factors causally reduce or increase consumption.
engineering_relevance: The results support practical decisions in trolleybus operation and network planning, including driving guidance, vehicle allocation, battery-catenary scheduling, and catenary infrastructure design.
method: Periodic time encoding + parameter-efficient batch ensemble for mixed static and sequential inputs
Bayesian optimization with tree-structured density estimation for hyperparameter tuning
Three-stage causal pipeline: feature attribution, LiNGAM causal direction discovery, and meta-learner treatment effects
evidence_signal: On the Zurich trolleybus dataset with weather data, the method achieved 6.52% MAPE and R = 0.982, outperforming ten statistical, tree-ensemble, and deep learning baselines.
watch_item: Causal findings are observational and depend on model assumptions and data quality, so the identified drivers should be validated before using them as operational policies.
paper_title: Inter-Stop Energy Prediction and Causal Driver Quantification for Dual-Source Trolleybuses via a Time-Aware Tabular Deep Learning Architecture
